# MadeManifest Engine — Trinity v1

The Trinity v1 release.  The engine implements the
[`specifications/trinity/`](specifications/trinity/) contract end-to-end across
fifteen migration phases (0..14): canonical input validation
(Document 04), canonical output envelope (Document 07), canonical
calculation pipeline (Document 05), canonical mapping
(Document 06), determinism and versioning rules (Document 08), and
the Phase 11 Golden Test Pack (Document 10) — all wired through a
hardened HTTP service deployable on Docker and Kubernetes.

This is a **conditional-acceptance release**: every required
golden test passes on local subprocess, Docker container, and kind
cluster, but the eight canon-ambiguity items A1..A8 carry
`UNRESOLVED` status pending canon-owner review (the engine ships
documented working assumptions for each).

## Version pins

| Field                  | Value              |
|------------------------|--------------------|
| `engine_version`       | `v1.0.0-trinity`  |
| `canon_version`        | `trinity-v1-rev-0` |
| `mapping_version`      | `trinity-v1-rev-0` |
| `input_schema_version` | `trinity-v1-rev-0` |
| `source_stack_version` | `trinity-v1-rev-0` |
| `swisseph_version`     | `2.10.03`          |
| `tzdb_version`         | `2023c`            |

The seven values above are exposed at runtime via `GET /version`,
plus the diagnostic field `ephe_path_resolved` (the
deployment-resolved Swiss Ephemeris data directory).  Trinity
response `metadata` carries the five canon-pinned fields only.

## Highlights

- **Trinity HTTP contract.**  `POST /manifest` accepts the
  canonical 5-field payload and returns either a Trinity success
  envelope or a Trinity error envelope.  The handler enforces
  `Content-Type: application/json` (415 + `invalid_input`), caps
  bodies at 1 MiB (413 + `unsupported_input`), classifies
  malformed JSON as 400 + `invalid_input`, and surfaces every
  rejection category through a stable status-code policy.
- **Canonical calculation pipeline.**  Tropical-zodiac astrology
  (signs, houses, 13 objects, angles), pure-bisection
  design-time solver (canon constants compiled in, backward-only
  search, 1-second canonical convergence), mandala mapping at
  the canonical 277.5° anchor, full structural derivation
  (channels, centers, definition, type, authority, profile,
  incarnation cross), and Gene Keys derivation from HD pillars.
- **23-fixture Trinity Golden Test Pack.**  3 valid_baseline +
  5 valid_edge + 5 invalid_input + 5 incomplete_input +
  2 unsupported_input + 3 regression_sentinel cases under
  `src/golden/trinity/<category>/<name>/{input,expected}.json`,
  driven end-to-end through `pkg/golden` against all three
  runtimes via `TestTrinityGoldenPack`.
- **Three-runtime test harness.**  Build-tag-gated subprocess /
  Docker container / kind cluster runners; the same fixture set
  exercises every layer of the stack on every CI run.
- **Boot-time canon self-check.**  `pkg/canon.SelfCheck()`
  validates every compiled-in canonical constant (GateOrder
  permutation, ChannelTable well-formedness, MandalaAnchorDeg
  range, etc.) and `ephemeris.ValidateEphePath()` verifies the
  resolved data directory exists; either failure is fatal.
- **Determinism guard.**  No mutable caches, no implicit
  environment defaults, no third-party service calls, no stored
  run history.  The retired `SE_NODE_POLICY` env shim is
  bit-for-bit immune across local / docker / kind harnesses.

## Container and Kubernetes hardening (Phase 13)

| Control                          | Source                                  |
|----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| `golang:1.22-bookworm` digest    | `sha256:3d699e4d…d55a253` (linux/amd64) |
| `debian:bookworm-slim` digest    | `sha256:f9c6a2fd…d2d5c252` (linux/amd64) |
| `USER 10001:10001`               | `src/Dockerfile`                        |
| `HEALTHCHECK CMD curl /healthz`  | `src/Dockerfile`                        |
| pod `runAsNonRoot`, UID 10001    | `src/deploy/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| `seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault` | `src/deploy/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false` | `src/deploy/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`   | `src/deploy/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| `capabilities.drop: [ALL]`       | `src/deploy/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| NetworkPolicy → ingress-nginx    | `src/deploy/kubernetes/networkpolicy.yaml` |
| HPA: 1..10 replicas, 70% CPU     | `src/deploy/kubernetes/hpa.yaml`        |

## Phase summary

| Phase | Done |
|-------|------|
| 0 | Three-runtime test harness + CI matrix |
| 1 | `pkg/canon` constants, version pins, `GET /version`, `--version` |
| 2 | Trinity input validator + nine-case rejection matrix |
| 3 | Success/error envelope types + typed marshalers + status-code policy |
| 4 | Trinity astrology pipeline (signs, houses, 13 objects, angles) |
| 5 | Design-time bisection solver (forbidden-shortcut sentinel) |
| 6 | Mandala mapping with canonical 277.5° anchor; `SE_NODE_POLICY` retired |
| 7 | Structural derivations (channels, centers, definition, type, authority, profile, cross) |
| 8 | Canonical Gene Keys block; `lifes_work` → `life_work` rename completed |
| 9 | Determinism cleanup: `canon.SelfCheck()`, `ephe_path_resolved`, env-immunity |
| 10 | HTTP contract finalised: content-type, oversize, malformed-JSON paths |
| 11 | Golden Test Pack: 23 fixtures across 6 canon categories |
| 12 | PoC contract retired: 7 packages, file-based CLI, legacy canon JSON deleted |
| 13 | Deployment hardening: digest pins, non-root, NetworkPolicy, HPA |
| 14 | Release + acceptance: this tag |

## Test coverage at the release commit

| Leg                       | Wall time |
|---------------------------|-----------|
| `go test ./pkg/...`       | ≈ 1.5 s   |
| `make test-integration-local`  | 3.1 s |
| `make test-integration-docker` | 4.5 s |
| `make test-integration-k8s`    | 44.9 s |

CI run `24936196695` confirmed every leg green on
`ubuntu-latest` (unit 40 s, validate-kubernetes 9 s, local 51 s,
docker 1 m 30 s, k8s 2 m 24 s).

A new nightly workflow (`.github/workflows/trinity-nightly.yml`)
runs the full pack at 04:30 UTC daily, captures per-leg logs +
base-image digests + the engine version, and uploads everything
alongside the standing acceptance report as a 90-day artefact.

## Conditional acceptance — open canon items

All eight register entries from
[`specifications/trinity/report.org`](specifications/trinity/report.org)
remain `UNRESOLVED` at this release.  The engine ships working
assumptions documented in
[`src/doc/version-pins.org`](src/doc/version-pins.org) and tagged in
code with the matching `A*` identifier.  Resolution of any item
that touches a compiled-in constant requires a `canon_version`
bump and a fresh Golden Pack capture.

| ID | Topic                                  |
|----|----------------------------------------|
| A1 | IANA tzdb release pin                  |
| A2 | House interval ownership rules         |
| A3 | Design-time second-selection rule      |
| A4 | Error-message catalog                  |
| A5 | `unsupported_input` boundary           |
| A6 | IANA link names                        |
| A7 | Golden first-fixture oracle approval   |
| A8 | Doc 05/06/07 consistency pass          |

## Verification

```bash
git checkout v1.0.0-trinity

# Build the production image and inspect its digest.
docker build -t mademanifest-engine:v1.0.0-trinity src/
docker images --no-trunc mademanifest-engine

# Confirm the engine's compiled-in versions.
make -C src compile
src/mademanifest-engine/mademanifest-engine --version

# Re-run the full test matrix.
make -C src test-unit
make -C src test-integration-local
make -C src test-integration-docker
make -C src test-integration-k8s
```

Boot-time gates (`canon.SelfCheck()` + `ephemeris.ValidateEphePath()`)
mean the engine refuses to start on a tampered canon or a missing
ephemeris path, so a corrupted build aborts immediately rather
than silently serving non-canonical results.

## Breaking changes since the pre-Trinity PoC

Phase 12 removed the legacy file-based CLI, the legacy canon JSON
loader, the legacy `case_id` / `engine_contract` / `expected`
input shape, and the seven retired Go packages
(`pkg/emit_golden`, `pkg/process_input`, `pkg/engine`,
`pkg/human_design`, `pkg/gene_keys`, `pkg/astrology`,
`pkg/geolocation`).  The HTTP service is the only runtime surface;
clients posting the old PoC payload to `POST /manifest` get a
Trinity `invalid_input` envelope.  The `CANON_DIRECTORY` env
variable is silently ignored.
